title: Podcast Hosts Say Goodbye After 10 Years
summary: Audrey and Tolly, the hosts of The Receipts Podcast, are stepping down after 10 years. They said this was a very hard decision that made them cry a lot. The podcast started in 2016 to let black women talk about many topics, and they made almost 300 episodes. They also had a popular show on the BBC and a deal with Spotify. Even though they were successful, they feel it is time to stop hosting to try new things. The podcast will not end; new hosts will take over while Audrey and Tolly help from behind the scenes. They plan to find new hosts through a search like a talent show, looking for fresh voices and ideas, and they want to help people learn even if they do not get picked.

working without being seen, like helping backstage

What will happen to the podcast after Audrey and Tolly step down?
How do they plan to find new hosts?

background_read: The Receipts Podcast is a popular show that started in 2016, created by Audrey Indome and Tolly Shoneye. It was made to give black women a place to talk openly about many things like life, culture, and news. Over 10 years, they made almost 300 episodes, became successful with deals like one with Spotify, and even had a BBC show that was number one. Now, the hosts are leaving to try new adventures, but they want the podcast to continue with new people, helping them learn and grow.
Article_Structure: WHO: Audrey Indome and Tolly Shoneye, the hosts of The Receipts Podcast
WHAT: They are stepping down after 10 years of hosting, but the podcast will continue with new hosts
WHERE: The podcast is based online and had a BBC spin-off, with a deal from Spotify
WHY: They feel it is time to stop hosting to evolve and try new things, and they want to help new voices join the show

perspectives.description: Audrey and Tolly describe leaving as a difficult, emotional decision that made them cry, showing their deep connection to the podcast.
